summary: Stancil argues that “founder mode” is mostly a new set of rules masquerading as contrarian wisdom, and that copying Jobs-style leadership is just another cargo cult. The deeper point is that there aren’t real rules—effective leadership is doing something bold and making it work, i.e., “getting away with it.”
money_quotes: Computers don’t tie us down; we do.
Our rules are insurgent, but they are rules, and they are also hard to break.
Founder mode is not advice; it’s something to yell when you bulldoze into a meeting and make the decision yourself.
Founder mode is not the answer to that question; it is simply permission to look for a solution.
You can do it, if you can get away with it.
key_insight: The real lesson of “founder mode” isn’t a new playbook but the permission to choose bold, unconventional approaches—and only success makes them legitimate.
